name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD materials chemist with 6+ years in analytical characterization, stability/degradation analysis, thermal/spectroscopic methods, and DOE-guided process-property studies. Brings a materials-science lens to formulation, particle/biophysical characterization, technical reports, and cross-functional CMC problem solving while transparently bridging into biologics-specific methods.

education.details:
Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials
Advisor: Prof. Seth Marder
(Transferred from Georgia Institute of Technology)

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science
education.details: INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education (Top 1% nationally)

bullets: Lead DOE-guided polymer process-development studies, defining analytical readouts, process windows, reproducibility criteria, and SOP-ready specifications for robust material quality.
Connect formulation/process variables with FT-IR, thermal-analysis, and spectroscopy data to support stability interpretation, troubleshooting, and technical recommendations.
Characterized 15+ functional material variants using UV-Vis-NIR, FT-IR, cyclic voltammetry, spectroelectrochemistry, NMR, GPC, TGA, DSC, and MS-informed analysis.
Mapped degradation and stability failure modes through hypothesis-driven experiments, orthogonal characterization, and rigorous technical documentation.
Developed molecular thin-film coating processes and linked CVD/surface-preparation variables with morphology, interface quality, and material performance.
Optimized CVD polymer thin-film deposition with DOE strategies and in-situ QCM-D monitoring to quantify growth kinetics and improve reproducibility.

Managed characterization/process-development lab operations for a 15-member group; implemented equipment tracking, calibration routines, SOPs, and safety protocols that improved instrument uptime by 30% and strengthened data integrity.

projects.title: Stability and Degradation Characterization
projects.description: Mapped material stability and degradation mechanisms using orthogonal spectroscopy, electrochemistry, and thermal analysis.
projects.tech: FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, CV, DSC/TGA, NMR, GPC, MS

projects.title: Process-Property Method Development
projects.description: Built analytical readouts and process windows that connect material attributes to reproducible specifications.
projects.tech: DOE, SPC, FT-IR, thermal analysis, SOPs

projects.title: Instrumentation and Lab Operations
projects.description: Managed calibration, SOP, safety, and equipment systems supporting reliable multi-user characterization workflows.

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.

Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Beyond Rhodamine B: Investigating Alternative Xanthene Cores for Modular Electrochromic Systems." (Manuscript under preparation).
conferences: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials, ACS Fall National Meeting, Denver, CO, August 2024.
Electrochemical and Spectroscopic Properties of Substituted Rhodamine Derivatives, Gerischer-Lewerenz Electrochemistry Symposium, Fort Collins, CO, August 2024.
Decoupling Redox and Color for High-Contrast Electrochromics, Excited State Processes Conference, Santa Fe, NM, June 2023.
Electrochromism: Decoupling Color and Redox in Rhodamine-Based Systems, Rocky Mountain Solid State Chemistry Workshop, Boulder, CO, January 2023.

skills.Biophysical/Materials Characterization: FT-IR/IR, UV-Vis-NIR, spectroelectrochemistry, TGA, DSC, NMR, GPC/SEC, QCM-D, GC-MS/LC-MS exposure; DLS/CD/AUC familiarity to be developed
skills.Stability & Formulation Mindset: Material degradation, aggregation/stability concepts, process-composition-property relationships, particle-method development mindset, troubleshooting
skills.Process & Data Methods: DOE, SPC, JMP/basic, Excel, Python/basic, reproducibility studies, method comparison, technical reports
skills.Execution: SOPs, lab notebooks, instrument troubleshooting, calibration tracking, cross-functional communication, publication-quality writing

honors: Plastic Sustainability Innovation in Material Award

teaching.role: Teaching Assistant, Organic Chemistry I & Lab
teaching.description: Mentored 120+ undergraduates; developed supplemental materials improving exam performance by 15%.
